title: MCP-TRUTH: Single-source-of-truth MCP architecture
boundary: MCP subsystem: env safety, state consolidation, tool registration, reconnect, port allocation, status projection, error typing — ~1053 lines of mcp/index.ts plus session/tools.ts, desktop/ipc-config.ts, and app/server-sync.tsx
consumer_purpose: Eliminate 10 failure classes (F01/F1-F10), split truth sources, listener/env leaks, and zero core test coverage in the MCP subsystem

codesign_conventions.ref_pattern: Ref<Map<string, McpServerState>> with clone-before-mutate (new Map(map)). Use SynchronizedRef if concurrent access contention is high.
codesign_conventions.error_pattern: Schema.TaggedErrorClass for Effect error channel; NamedError.create() for boundary errors
codesign_conventions.retry_pattern: Effect.retry({ times: 3, schedule: Schedule.exponential('100 millis'), while: predicate })
codesign_conventions.port_pattern: Try preferred port; on EADDRINUSE, fall back to port 0 for unconfigured defaults. Fail on explicit port conflict.
codesign_conventions.service_pattern: Interface + Service (Context.Service tag) + layer (Layer.effect) + defaultLayer (fully provided). Export * as Namespace.
codesign_conventions.test_pattern: testEffect(layer) factory + it.effect runner. No mocks — real implementations with Layer.provide injection.
codesign_conventions.cleanup_pattern: Effect.addFinalizer for resource cleanup; Scope.addFinalizer for explicit scope management.

root_causes.hypothesis: process.env spread passes all host secrets to third-party MCP child processes
evidence: mcp/index.ts:456: env: { ...process.env, ... } in StdioClientTransport constructor
No filtering or whitelist — ALL env vars (API keys, tokens, secrets) leak to every MCP server process
discovery finding ses_1874379b: sanitizedProcessEnv() renamed but still a no-op delegation

root_causes.hypothesis: setNotificationHandler never removed on close — listener leak
evidence: mcp/index.ts:532: client.setNotificationHandler(ToolListChangedNotificationSchema, async () => {...})
mcp/index.ts:625-630: closeClient only calls client.close() + delete s.defs[name] — no removeNotificationHandler call
discovery finding ses_18303d46: LSP client has identical listener cleanup gap — systemic pattern

root_causes.hypothesis: 1053-line core module has zero dedicated test files
evidence: Only OAuth sub-modules tested: oauth-provider.test.ts, oauth-browser.test.ts, oauth-auto-connect.test.ts
5 regression-proof tests reference MCP but test external behavior, not internal lifecycle
No connect/disconnect lifecycle tests, no env sanitization tests, no listener cleanup tests

root_causes.hypothesis: 4 .pipe(Effect.ignore) sites silently swallow transport close failures
evidence: mcp/index.ts:322: (t, exit) => (Exit.isFailure(exit) ? Effect.tryPromise(() => t.close()).pipe(Effect.ignore) : Effect.void)
mcp/index.ts:405: .pipe(Effect.ignore, Effect.as(...)) in toast notification
mcp/index.ts:625: Effect.tryPromise(() => client.close()).pipe(Effect.ignore) in closeClient
Stale client references remain in State after silent close failure

root_causes.hypothesis: MCP registration failures in ACP bridge go unreported
evidence: mcp.add() returns Effect but callers don't handle the error channel
No McpFailed error propagation to ACP response

root_causes.hypothesis: Tool list cached until reconnect or notification — staleness window
evidence: mcp/index.ts:200: defs() returns tool list, cached in s.defs[name]
mcp/index.ts:531-557: watch() updates on ToolListChanged notification only
No periodic refresh or TTL-based invalidation

root_causes.hypothesis: Transport failure = permanent failure — no auto-reconnect
evidence: mcp/index.ts:311-323: connectTransport uses Effect.acquireRelease with single attempt
Any transport error during start() results in permanent failed status
PgPool has retry pattern (storage/pg-pool.ts:100) — inconsistency

root_causes.hypothesis: OAuth callback port 19876 hardcoded — two concurrent OAuth MCP servers conflict
evidence: oauth-provider.ts:14: const OAUTH_CALLBACK_PORT = 19876
oauth-provider.ts:42: const port = this.config.callbackPort ?? OAUTH_CALLBACK_PORT
No EADDRINUSE handling or fallback allocation
server.ts:120-125 has explicit port fallback pattern — inconsistency

root_causes.hypothesis: MCP tools bypass ToolRegistry — direct assignment in SessionTools
evidence: session/tools.ts:160-248: for (const [key, item] of Object.entries(yield* mcp.tools())) { ... tools[key] = item }
MCP tools never pass through ToolRegistry.Service
No unified tool lifecycle: built-in tools go through registry.tools(), MCP tools don't

root_causes.hypothesis: Desktop reads MCP status from electron-store; runtime reads from MCP.State — split truth
evidence: desktop/ipc-config.ts:71-77: getStore('desktop-mcp-servers') reads from electron-store
mcp/index.ts:248-253: State.status Map is the runtime truth
No synchronization between electron-store (persisted config) and Effect runtime status
Desktop shows 'connected' while runtime shows 'failed' during transport errors

root_causes.hypothesis: Duck-typed error detection misses structured MCP SDK errors
evidence: session/tools.ts:189: const mcpStatus = (result as { isError?: boolean })?.isError ? 'failed' : 'succeeded'
MCP SDK CallToolResult has isError but no structured error codes captured
No McpToolResult wrapper — raw SDK type passed through

phases.name: Type Foundation
phases.description: Add McpServerState, McpErrorCode, McpToolResult, ALLOWED_MCP_ENV — zero behavioral change, pure type additions
phases.before: No McpServerState, no McpErrorCode enum, no McpToolResult typed wrapper, no env whitelist constant
phases.after: McpServerState Schema.Class with 17 fields, McpErrorCode Schema.Literal union (8 variants), McpToolResult Schema.Struct, ALLOWED_MCP_ENV Set<string>
risks: None — pure type additions, no behavioral impact
validation: bun typecheck in packages/opencode

phases.name: Env Sanitization
phases.description: Replace ...process.env with whitelist-filtered env in StdioClientTransport — blocks F01 secret leak

callers_affected: All MCP server connections via StdioClientTransport

risks: Some MCP servers may need env vars not in whitelist — mitigation: mcp.environment allows explicit pass-through
Consider expanding whitelist after gathering real-world MCP server env requirements (NODE_ENV, PYTHONUNBUFFERED, VIRTUAL_ENV)
validation: Test: process.env.SECRET_TOKEN NOT passed to child process
Test: HOME, PATH, USER ARE passed
Test: mcp.environment custom vars ARE passed even if not in whitelist

phases.name: Single State Ref Migration
phases.description: Replace parallel maps (config, status, clients, defs) with Ref<Map<string, McpServerState>> — eliminates split truth and module-level mutable state
phases.before: interface State { config: Record<string, ConfigMCP.Info>, status: Record<string, Status>, clients: Record<string, MCPClient>, defs: Record<string, MCPToolDef[]> }
phases.after: const stateRef = Ref.make<Map<string, McpServerState>>(new Map()) — all access via Ref.get/Ref.update with clone-before-mutate
phases.convention_alignment: Matches HealthRegistry pattern (Ref<Map<K,V>>) and Plugin Registry pattern (clone-before-mutate: new Map(map)). SynchronizedRef considered but Ref sufficient since MCP operations are serialized via serverLocks.

tests_to_run: new lifecycle test: create → connect → disconnect

risks: High surface area: 15-20 call sites must be adapted atomically — cannot have half old State, half new Ref
Concurrency semantics change: parallel record access was synchronous; Ref access is Effect-wrapped — requires yield* at every access point
serverLocks Map and pendingOAuthTransports Map must also move into Ref (module-level mutable state elimination)
validation: bun typecheck — all Ref access patterns must type-check
Existing regression proofs: mcp_configured_servers_visible_in_desktop_status, mcp_processes_are_deduplicated_per_scope
New lifecycle test: create server → connect → verify status=connected → disconnect → verify status=disconnected

phases.name: Listener Cleanup + Silent Failure Fix
phases.description: closeClient must unregister all notification handlers, verify listenerCount→0, and replace 4 Effect.ignore sites with logged errors

before.closeClient: function closeClient(s: State, name: string) { const client = s.clients[name]; delete s.defs[name]; if (!client) return Effect.void; return Effect.tryPromise(() => client.close()).pipe(Effect.ignore) }

after.closeClient: Removes notification handler via client.setNotificationHandler(ToolListChangedNotificationSchema, null); verifies listenerCount→0; updates State atomically via Ref.update; logs close errors instead of swallowing
after.silent_failures: Each .pipe(Effect.ignore) replaced with Effect.catchAll((e) => Effect.logError('[MCP] close failed', e)) — logs but doesn't fail the Effect

risks: setNotificationHandler(null) behavior depends on MCP SDK version — verify it clears rather than throws
Logging close errors may produce noise for expected shutdowns — use Log.debug for clean disconnects
validation: Test: connect MCP → verify listenerCount > 0 → disconnect → verify listenerCount = 0
Test: connect MCP → kill transport process → verify close error is logged (not swallowed)
Test: connect/disconnect cycle 10 times → verify no listener accumulation (memory leak prevention)

phases.name: Tool Registry Integration
phases.description: MCP tools register/unregister through ToolRegistry; SessionTools reads MCP tools from registry, not direct assignment

before.mcp_tool_registration: session/tools.ts:160-248: for (const [key, item] of Object.entries(yield* mcp.tools())) { tools[key] = item } — bypasses ToolRegistry

after.toolregistry_interface: Adds: registerExternal(tools: Tool.Info[]): Effect<void>; unregisterExternal(serverId: string): Effect<void>; external tools stored in InstanceState<State> as external: Map<string, Tool.Info[]>
after.mcp_connect: On connect: yield* toolRegistry.registerExternal(tools). On disconnect: yield* toolRegistry.unregisterExternal(name). On tool list change notification: unregister then re-register.
after.session_tools: MCP tools appear in yield* registry.tools() output — no separate mcp.tools() loop needed

phases.tool_id_convention: MCP tool IDs prefixed with server name: ${serverId}__${toolName} to prevent collision with built-in tools

tests_to_run: registry: MCP tools appear in ToolRegistry
registry: tool list change → tools updated
regression: built-in tools still resolve correctly

risks: Breaking change: mcp.tools() currently returns Record<string, Tool> — must preserve execute wrapper during ToolRegistry registration
Permission model: MCP tools ask permission inline in execute wrapper; ToolRegistry tools use Permission.ask at call time — must align
If Permission.ask differences exist, keep the MCP-specific execute wrapper in ToolRegistry for MCP tools
validation: Test: connect MCP server → tools appear in registry.tools()
Test: disconnect MCP server → tools removed from registry.tools()
Test: tool list change notification → tools updated in registry
Regression: existing built-in tools still resolve correctly

phases.name: Auto-Reconnect
phases.description: Transport failure triggers up to 3 retries with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s); McpServerState.restartCount tracks attempts
phases.convention_alignment: Matches PgPool retry pattern: Effect.retry({ times: 3, schedule: Schedule.exponential('100 millis'), while: predicate }). Uses identical shape.
phases.before: Effect.acquireRelease with single attempt — any transport error = permanent failure
phases.after: Effect.retry wrapping transport.start() with times: 3, Schedule.exponential('1 second'), while: (e) => e.code !== 'CONFIG_INVALID' (don't retry config errors). RestartCount updated per attempt.

tests_to_run: reconnect: success on 2nd attempt → status=connected, restartCount=1

risks: Stdio transport spawns new process on each retry — must close previous transport before retry to prevent zombie processes
Rapid reconnect could overwhelm remote MCP servers — exponential backoff mitigates
Interaction with Phase 3 Ref state: restartCount must be written atomically via Ref.update
validation: Test: simulate transport failure → verify retry with backoff timing (1s, 2s, 4s)
Test: 3 failures → permanent failure with errorCode=CONNECTION_FAILED
Test: success on 2nd attempt → status=connected, restartCount=1

phases.name: Dynamic Port Allocation
phases.description: OAuth callback: try 19876 first; on EADDRINUSE, fall back to random port for unconfigured defaults. Explicit config ports fail with PORT_CONFLICT error.
phases.convention_alignment: Matches server.ts:120-125 port fallback pattern. EADDRINUSE classification matches desktop/sidecar-startup-truth.test.ts:19 pattern.
phases.before: const port = this.config.callbackPort ?? OAUTH_CALLBACK_PORT — always 19876, no fallback
phases.after: allocateCallbackPort(preferred): tries preferred port via net.createServer().listen(); on EADDRINUSE with no explicit config → try port 0 (OS-assigned); on EADDRINUSE with explicit config → fail with PortConflictError. Allocated port stored in McpServerState.

dual_behavior.default_unconfigured: Try 19876 → EADDRINUSE → OS-assigned random port (port 0). Redirect URI updated to match.
dual_behavior.explicitly_configured: Fail on EADDRINUSE with McpErrorCode.PORT_CONFLICT. User must resolve conflict manually (OAuth provider won't match dynamic port).

tests_to_run: port: default port allocated, fallback on conflict
port: explicit port fails on conflict

risks: Dynamic port changes redirect URI — OAuth provider must accept different URIs per session. Mitigation: only use dynamic port for unconfigured defaults; fail explicitly on configured port conflicts where the OAuth provider has a pre-registered URI.
net.createServer() is a non-Effect side effect in the provider constructor. Should be wrapped as Effect and called during service initialization.
validation: Test: two concurrent MCP servers with default OAuth → different ports allocated
Test: explicit callbackPort → fails cleanly on conflict with PORT_CONFLICT error
Test: allocated port matches redirect URI returned by McpOAuthProvider.redirectUrl

phases.name: Status Projection Fix
phases.description: Desktop reads MCP status from MCP.State via Bus events (push model), not electron-store polling

before.desktop_read: ipc-config.ts:71-77: getStore('desktop-mcp-servers') reads from electron-store — stale, desyncs from runtime
before.app_poll: server-sync.tsx:49-54: loadMcpQuery polls SDK for MCP status — relies on bridge

after.bus_event: New McpStatusChanged BusEvent emitted on every status transition in mcp/index.ts (connect, disconnect, fail, reconnect)
after.desktop_read: Desktop GET_MCP_STATUS IPC handler bridges to runtime MCP.Service.status() with 1s cache TTL. Or: Desktop subscribes to McpStatusChanged via existing Bus bridge.
after.app_sync: server-sync.tsx subscribes to McpStatusChanged instead of polling loadMcpQuery. Invalidation on config writes.

affected_files: desktop/ipc-config.ts (new IPC handler or bus subscription)

risks: IPC bridge overhead for every status read — mitigate with 1s cache TTL in IPC handler
Race condition: config write (electron-store) and status read (Effect runtime) could show stale config — config writes must invalidate status cache
validation: Test: modify MCP config in Desktop UI → status reflects change immediately
Test: kill MCP process → Desktop shows 'failed' within 2s
Test: restart MCP process (auto-reconnect Phase 6) → Desktop shows 'connected' after reconnect

phases.name: Typed Error Handling
phases.description: Replace duck-typed (result as { isError?: boolean })?.isError with Schema.decodeUnknownSync(McpToolResult)
phases.before: session/tools.ts:189: const mcpStatus = (result as { isError?: boolean })?.isError ? 'failed' : 'succeeded'
phases.after: const decoded = Schema.decodeUnknownSync(McpToolResult)(result); const mcpStatus = decoded.isError ? 'failed' : 'succeeded'; const errorCode = decoded.errorCode // typed McpErrorCode, not string

tests_to_run: errors: typed errorCode on connection failure

risks: Schema.decodeUnknownSync adds overhead per MCP tool call — O(1) on small structs, negligible
MCP SDK CallToolResult may have fields not covered by McpToolResult — start with SDK-matching schema, extend as needed
validation: Test: successful MCP tool call → isError=false, errorCode=undefined
Test: failed MCP tool call → isError=true, errorCode set to correct McpErrorCode variant
TypeScript: zero as { isError?: boolean } casts remaining in MCP code paths

phases.name: Core Tests
phases.description: 17+ tests for MCP lifecycle, env safety, listener cleanup, reconnect, port allocation, tool registry, status projection, and error typing

test_infrastructure.transports: FakeStdioTransport and FakeHTTPTransport (in-process, no real child process spawning)
test_infrastructure.config_override: withTmpdirInstance({ config: { mcp: { testServer: { type: 'local', command: ['echo', 'test'] } } } })

test_cases.name: env: process.env secrets not leaked to child
cleanup: close errors logged, not swallowed
cleanup: 10x connect/disconnect cycle, no listener accumulation
port: default port allocated, fallback on EADDRINUSE
port: explicit port fails with PORT_CONFLICT on EADDRINUSE
registry: MCP tools appear in ToolRegistry.tools()
registry: tools removed from ToolRegistry on disconnect
errors: McpToolResult.errorCode typed on connection failure

risk_assessment.risk: Phase 3 Ref migration regresses connection handling
risk_assessment.mitigation: Atomic commit; run all existing regression proofs before landing; keep old State interface in git stash for fast rollback

risk_assessment.risk: Env whitelist breaks legitimate MCP server setups
risk_assessment.mitigation: mcp.environment allows explicit pass-through; document migration path; expand whitelist after gathering real-world needs
Preserve MCP permission-ask in execute wrapper; test with ask-before-execute workflow; keep MCP-specific execute wrapper in ToolRegistry

risk_assessment.risk: Dynamic port breaks OAuth redirect URI matching
risk_assessment.mitigation: Only use dynamic port for unconfigured defaults; fail explicitly on configured port conflicts; OAuth provider must accept dynamic defaults

risk_assessment.risk: Status projection bridges introduce IPC latency
risk_assessment.mitigation: Cache with 1s TTL in IPC handler; use push (Bus) model over polling
Close previous transport before retry; add process cleanup on scope close; verify no zombie processes in tests
Verify the MCP SDK version supports null handler; fall back to no-op handler if null unsupported

rollback_plan: Each phase is independently revertible in reverse order. Most complex: Phase 3 (keep old State interface in git stash for fast restoration). Phase 1 types can be kept even if rolled back (zero behavioral impact).
